diagnosis: vwap_reversion is a symmetric mean-reversion strategy that fires when price deviates more than vwap_deviation_pct (default 1.5%) from a 20-bar volume-weighted average price, confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold and optionally a volume spike and wick-rejection filter. It emits BOTH long (price below VWAP + RSI < 30) and short (price above VWAP + RSI > 70) signals. The ADX filter (adx_max=30) correctly limits entries to low-trend / ranging environments. The take-profit target is set exactly at VWAP itself, so the expected move per trade is exactly the VWAP deviation at entry — mechanically ~1.5-3% per trade. The stop is ATR-based (2x ATR below/above entry). The strategy was pruned from the Phase-2 STRATEGY_REGISTRY in Round 1 after 10 hours / 29K evaluations with 0% positive fitness. It has NO active param_space entry. The failure in Phase 2 is almost certainly because: (a) the symmetric short branch was useful only when perp-funding shorts could offset carry, and without that edge the short side in a spot-long environment bleeds on crypto's secular upward bias; (b) a 1.5% VWAP deviation on a 20-bar rolling VWAP is a very low threshold that triggers frequently during volatile crypto moves, leading to catching falling knives on the long side and chasing false reversions on the short side; (c) the RSI-30 oversold threshold combined with ADX-30 maximum creates a regime that perfectly coincides with sharp directional sell-offs where mean reversion does NOT reliably occur. The long-only edge is structurally possible — oversold bounces in ranging/lateral regimes are real — but requires substantial refactoring to suppress the short branch and add stronger quality filters.
long_only_hypothesis: In crypto spot markets, prices that temporarily trade well below their rolling VWAP (~20 bars) during a regime of compressed volatility and lateral price action tend to revert toward VWAP within 12-48 hours. This reversion is driven by passive market-making and algorithmic orders anchored to VWAP, combined with panic-selling exhaustion measured by RSI and buying-pressure microstructure. The edge is long-or-cash: go long only when price is oversold relative to VWAP in a non-trending regime; exit to cash when price reaches VWAP or the trade degrades. The short branch is fully dropped. A higher VWAP-deviation threshold (2.0-4.0%) combined with confirming OBV divergence and a bb_bw_percentile compression gate should improve trade quality and reduce false signals in directional downtrends. Regime gate using bmsb_bullish or price above 20-week SMA ensures we only enter longs when the macro structure is constructive.
market_techniques: Mean-reversion entry off rolling VWAP in lateral/compressed-vol regimes
Dual confirmation: RSI oversold + volume spike (panic selling exhaustion)
Microstructure filter: buying_pressure (lower wick rejection) as entry quality gate
ADX ceiling filter to avoid catching falling knives in trending downturns
OBV divergence as secondary confirmation that sellers are exhausting
Bollinger Band compression (bb_bw_percentile) to identify range-bound regimes suitable for mean reversion
Bull Market Support Band (bmsb_bullish) as a macro regime gate — longs only when price is above the long-period SMA/EMA band
Volatility-scaled position sizing (ATR-normalized) to reduce size in high-volatility environments
Time-stop (24-48h) to prevent dead-money trades in unresponsive ranges
Trailing stop once price approaches VWAP to lock in partial gains
useful_features: mean-reversion: bb_pct_b (price position within Bollinger Band; entry when bb_pct_b < 0.2)
volatility: bb_bw_percentile (regime gate — prefer entries when percentile < 40, i.e., volatility is compressed)
volatility: atr_14 (stop placement and position sizing)
volatility: gk_vol (secondary vol regime filter; low gk_vol favors reversion)
momentum: rsi_14 (oversold filter, primary — already used)
momentum: rsi_7 (faster RSI for sharper signal; look for divergence between rsi_7 and rsi_14)
momentum: ret_zscore_20 (z-score of recent 1-bar return; deep negative z-score confirms oversold)
momentum: price_acceleration (negative acceleration helps confirm exhaustion)
volume: volume_zscore (complementary volume abnormality measure; >2.0 sigma spike on down bars signals capitulation)
volume: obv_divergence (OBV rising while price falls = demand absorption)
microstructure: buying_pressure (wick rejection filter — already used)
microstructure: pressure_imbalance (combined buy/sell balance; positive imbalance at low prices confirms bounce)
trend: adx_14 (regime filter — ADX < 25 to confirm ranging/lateral)
trend: bmsb_bullish (macro long filter — only enter longs when price is above 20-SMA and 21-EMA band)
market regime (informative only): ret_1 rolling z-score as crypto risk-on/off proxy

genome_params.name: vwap_deviation_pct
genome_params.note: Minimum deviation below VWAP to qualify as oversold. Wider deviation = rarer but higher quality signals. Current 1.5% generates too many false signals.

genome_params.name: rsi_oversold
genome_params.note: RSI threshold for oversold. Tighter (20-25) = fewer but stronger signals; looser (30-38) = more trades.

genome_params.name: adx_max
genome_params.note: Maximum ADX to allow entry. Below this = lateral/range regime. Too low = too rare; too high = catches trending markets.

genome_params.name: volume_spike_threshold
genome_params.note: Volume ratio needed to confirm panic selling. Higher threshold selects higher-quality capitulation events.

genome_params.name: wick_ratio_min
genome_params.note: Minimum buying_pressure (lower-wick size relative to bar range) for entry. Confirms buyers absorbing at lows.

genome_params.name: stop_loss_atr_mult
genome_params.note: ATR multiple for stop-loss below entry price. Wider stop survives noise but increases loss magnitude.

genome_params.name: take_profit_atr_mult
genome_params.note: Used to set a hard TP ceiling above entry in addition to VWAP reversion target. Prevents leaving gains when VWAP is far.

genome_params.name: time_stop_hours
genome_params.note: Maximum hours to hold a non-moving reversion trade before exiting to preserve capital for next opportunity.

genome_params.name: require_volume_spike
genome_params.note: Toggle volume-spike requirement. Genome can evolve to drop the filter if data shows it does not add value.

genome_params.name: require_wick_rejection
genome_params.note: Toggle wick-rejection requirement. Allows evolution to evaluate whether microstructure filter improves results.

genome_params.name: bb_bw_pct_gate
genome_params.note: Maximum bb_bw_percentile allowed at entry. Below this threshold = volatility is compressed = favorable for reversion. New param not in original.

genome_params.name: require_bmsb_bullish
genome_params.note: Macro regime gate: only enter if bmsb_bullish = True (price above both 20-SMA and 21-EMA). Filters out bear markets.

do_not_optimize: rsi_overbought (short branch is removed; this param becomes irrelevant)
allowed_regimes (string list — optimize regime logic through quantitative feature gates, not string label matching which is regime-label-dependent)
require_regime boolean (replace with quantitative gates like adx_max and bb_bw_pct_gate that are directly testable)
Signal.strength formula (min(abs(deviation_pct)/3, 1.0) — linear scaling of a single threshold ratio; optimizing the divisor (3) and cap (1.0) independently creates false precision)

risk_management.stop: ATR-based stop below entry: stop_loss = entry_price - stop_loss_atr_mult * atr_14; default 2.0x ATR. Genome range 1.5-4.0x ATR. Hard minimum: close below prior 20-bar low also triggers stop.
risk_management.take_profit: Primary TP at vwap_20 (reversion target as coded). Secondary hard TP ceiling at entry + take_profit_atr_mult * atr_14 to avoid overshoot. If VWAP is >3x ATR above entry, use ATR-based TP instead.
risk_management.trailing: Optional trailing stop once unrealized gain exceeds 0.5x ATR: trail at entry + 0.3x ATR (locks minimum profit). This prevents the common failure mode of a full reversion followed by re-breakdown.
risk_management.vol_target: Volatility-scaled sizing: target 15% annualized vol contribution per trade. Size = equity * (vol_target / max(annual_vol_estimate, 0.05)). Cap at max_position_pct. Reduces size in high-vol regimes where reversion trades fail most.
risk_management.max_size: Maximum 20% of equity per trade (config.max_position_pct = 0.20). Mean reversion has negative-skew risk; smaller size limits ruin from a sustained directional move against the position.
risk_management.cooldown: Minimum 4 hours between trades (risk_manager min_interval). After a stop-loss event, mandatory 12-hour cooldown to avoid re-entering during a continued drawdown. Prevents the classic mean-reversion cascade failure.
risk_management.risk_off: Composite risk-off gate: (1) adx_14 > adx_max [trending market filter]; (2) bb_bw_percentile > bb_bw_pct_gate [volatility expansion filter]; (3) bmsb_bullish == False when require_bmsb_bullish is True [macro bear market filter]; (4) circuit breaker from RiskManager if equity drawdown exceeds max_drawdown_pct. All conditions must clear for a new long entry.

metrics: CAGR (annualized): primary return metric; target >15% for a meaningful edge over BTC buy-and-hold on pre-lockbox data

Sharpe ratio (annualized, risk-free=0): target >1.0 walk-forward; the strategy's mean-reversion nature means frequent small gains offset by occasional large losses — Sharpe separates true edge from noise
Sortino ratio: more relevant than Sharpe here because mean-reversion has asymmetric loss distribution; target >1.5
Maximum drawdown (%): critical for mean-reversion strategies that can suffer correlated stops in trending markets; target <25%
Calmar ratio (CAGR / maxDD): target >0.6 for a viable strategy given crypto volatility
Hit rate (%): mean-reversion strategies should have >55% win rate for positive expectancy; below 50% indicates the edge is gone
Average trade duration (hours): should cluster around 12-36 hours for VWAP reversion; excessive duration (>72h) indicates failed reversions held too long by time-stop
Turnover / trade frequency: count of trades per month; too low (<2/month) risks insufficient statistical significance in walk-forward windows; too high (>30/month) risks cost sensitivity
Exposure time (%): fraction of time in a position; for a mean-reversion strategy expect 10-30%; higher suggests the regime filter is too loose
Cost sensitivity: compute net Sharpe at 0bp, 5bp, 10bp, 20bp commission+slippage to verify edge survives realistic costs (crypto CEX ~5-10bps round-trip)
Walk-forward OOS degradation: target <50% Sharpe degradation from IS to OOS across folds; the Round 1 0% fitness suggests very high degradation currently
Bootstrap / Monte Carlo on trade returns: resample 1000x to get 95th-percentile Sharpe distribution; confirms whether observed IS Sharpe is statistically significant or a small-N artifact

Per-year stability: compute annual Sharpe for each calendar year 2019-2023 (pre-lockbox); strategy must show positive Sharpe in majority of years, especially in ranging years (2019, 2020 pre-rally, 2022-2023)
sweep_changes: Remove the short branch entirely: delete the elif block (lines 78-95 of vwap_reversion.py). All short signals must be suppressed; treat the overbought condition as a flat/cash signal only.

Add bb_bw_pct_gate parameter: gate entries on current[bb_bw_percentile] < bb_bw_pct_gate (new param, range 20-60). This ensures VWAP reversion is only traded when volatility is compressed, filtering out trending environments more precisely than ADX alone.
Add require_bmsb_bullish parameter: when True, only enter if current[bmsb_bullish] is True. This implements a macro regime gate preventing long entries during prolonged bear markets.
Widen vwap_deviation_pct range to 1.5-5.0 in the genome param_space (current default 1.5% is too tight for crypto; most valid capitulation events occur at 2.5-4% deviations).
Add obv_divergence_check: optional filter requiring obv_divergence > 0 (OBV above its 20-period SMA) even as price is below VWAP — this confirms demand absorption is occurring.
Add ret_zscore_gate: require ret_zscore_20 < -1.0 at entry to confirm the bar is a statistically unusual down move, reducing noise signals from routine 1.5% fluctuations.
Add take_profit_atr_mult to param_space (range 1.0-3.5) as an ATR-based ceiling TP in addition to the VWAP target, preventing the strategy from holding through a full overshoot.

Add the vwap_reversion entry to STRATEGY_REGISTRY with the new param_space after the above changes are implemented. Key params: vwap_deviation_pct (float, 1.5, 5.0), rsi_oversold (float, 20.0, 38.0), adx_max (float, 18.0, 35.0), volume_spike_threshold (float, 1.2, 3.0), wick_ratio_min (float, 0.3, 0.7), stop_loss_atr_mult (float, 1.5, 4.0), take_profit_atr_mult (float, 1.0, 3.5), time_stop_hours (int, 12, 72), require_volume_spike (bool), require_wick_rejection (bool), bb_bw_pct_gate (float, 20.0, 60.0), require_bmsb_bullish (bool).
Increase the minimum warm-up check from bar_idx < 50 to bar_idx < 120 to match the embargo_bars=120 used by WalkForwardValidator and ensure bb_bw_percentile (100-bar lookback) is fully populated.

Validate that body_wick_ratio (used in original code line 44) is available in the feature set — it IS computed in microstructure_features() as body_wick_ratio, but the long-branch currently filters on buying_pressure not body_wick_ratio, creating a naming inconsistency. Standardize to buying_pressure throughout.
methodological_risks: Round-1 0% positive fitness is a strong prior against this strategy: even with long-only conversion, the core mean-reversion edge on short-duration VWAP deviations may simply not exist in crypto at hourly granularity. The Phase-3 rework should be treated as hypothesis testing, not tuning.

VWAP computed over 20 bars is sensitive to timeframe: on 1h data it is a 20-hour rolling VWAP; on 4h it is an 80-hour VWAP. A 1.5% deviation threshold that is oversold on 4h may be trivial noise on 1h. The sweep must document the target timeframe and fix it.

Regime-label dependency risk: the original allowed_regimes=['lateral','mean_reversion'] depends on an external regime-classifier whose labels may not be stable across walk-forward folds. Converting to quantitative gates (ADX, bb_bw_percentile, bmsb_bullish) eliminates this hidden dependency.

Mean-reversion in crypto has structural asymmetry: prices revert from oversold more reliably during bull markets and fail catastrophically during bear-market capitulation events. Without the bmsb_bullish or similar macro filter, the strategy will accumulate its worst losses in exactly the worst market conditions.

Small-N problem: the RSI-30 + 1.5% VWAP deviation + volume spike triple filter produces very few signals, especially in trending markets. Walk-forward windows of 15 days may see 0-5 trades, making fold-level Sharpe estimates statistically meaningless. Minimum of 30 trades per fold should be enforced as a validity gate.

Take-profit at exactly VWAP creates a perverse incentive: if VWAP has drifted significantly during a downtrend, the TP may be 5-8% above entry while the stop is only 2 ATR below — this inverted R-multiple structure destroys expected value. The ATR-based secondary TP ceiling is essential.

Overfitting risk in the vwap_deviation_pct parameter: a sweep over 1.5-5.0% across many folds will find a value that maximizes IS Sharpe by selecting specific capitulation events that happened to recover. The OOS lockbox test (2024-2026) is the only honest evaluation of whether that threshold generalizes.

Correlation with regime detector: if the regime labels are generated from the same price data as the strategy features, there is a correlation-leakage risk when optimizing regime filters alongside VWAP parameters. Each parameter should be validated independently before combining in the genome.

Cost sensitivity is high for this strategy type: with an average expected move of 1.5-3% and round-trip costs of 10-20bps, the gross edge is thin (30-60x cost ratio). A small increase in slippage assumption from 5bps to 15bps could eliminate net profitability entirely. Must stress-test at 20bps round-trip.
